text.title: TDD Workflow — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every tdd-workflow application must obey.

rule.statement: Always write the failing test first. Run the suite; confirm RED. Only then write production code.
rule.rationale: Tests-after rationalise existing code; tests-first design the interface from the consumer's perspective.

rule.statement: Write the minimal code that makes the failing test pass. No speculative generality, no extras.
rule.rationale: Minimal code keeps the implementation honest; speculative code creates dead branches that decay.

rule.statement: Refactor only while tests stay green. Each refactor step ends with the suite green.
rule.rationale: Refactor-while-red conflates behaviour change with structure change; you cannot tell which broke things.

rule.statement: One failing assertion per RED step. If you need to assert multiple things, write multiple RED-GREEN cycles.
rule.rationale: Multi-assertion RED hides the true failure; you do not know which assertion the GREEN code actually addresses.

rule.statement: Never write code without a prior RED. If you skipped RED, you cannot trust the test is real.
rule.rationale: A test written after the code may pass trivially against the implementation it inspired; only a confirmed RED proves the test discriminates.
